title: CeraLive Add-on Descriptor
description: Descriptor for an optional, image-immutable CeraLive add-on. Each add-on ships as a systemd-sysext extension (.raw) that overlays ONLY /usr and /opt onto the running OS (lib/app-layer/sysext.sh). Descriptors are baked read-only into the image at /usr/share/ceralive/addons/<id>.json; CeraUI reads them at runtime to offer install/enable. The descriptor encodes the sysext merge identity the kernel keys on (sysextLevel/versionId, G1), the filesystem paths the extension provides (for cross-add-on collision detection, E6), and a provides/conflicts dependency model. appfs payloads are reserved and NOT implemented in this tier.

id.description: Unique add-on identifier and the descriptor's filename stem (e.g. 'debug-toolset' -> addons/debug-toolset.json, installed at /usr/share/ceralive/addons/debug-toolset.json). Referenced by other add-ons' deps[]/conflicts[]. Lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in the CeraUI add-on catalogue (e.g. 'Debug Toolset').

version.description: Add-on version as semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional -prerelease / +build). Drives upgrade comparison in the add-on manager.

category.description: Catalogue grouping used by the CeraUI add-on browser. Fixed vocabulary so the UI can render stable sections.

icon.description: Optional icon name or path for the CeraUI catalogue tile (icon-set name or relative asset path). Purely presentational.

payload.description: How the add-on materialises on the device. Only 'sysext' is implemented: a systemd-sysext extension that overlays /usr+/opt. 'appfs' is reserved for a future heavier-payload tier and is intentionally NOT accepted here.

type.description: Payload backend. Constrained to 'sysext' (appfs reserved/not implemented).

sysextLevel.description: G1 — the SYSEXT_LEVEL the extension-release file must carry so systemd's extension_release_validate() merges the .raw across minor VERSION_ID drift (lib/app-layer/sysext.sh: SYSEXT_LEVEL=1). MUST be the literal string '1'; any other value is un-mergeable on the device.

versionId.description: G1 — the VERSION_ID the extension-release file must carry to match the host os-release (Debian bookworm across the stack). MUST be the literal string '12'; the kernel refuses to merge an extension whose VERSION_ID does not match (lib/app-layer/sysext.sh: SYSEXT_OS_VERSION_ID=12).

compatibleOsVersions.description: OS VERSION_ID strings this add-on's artifact is published for (substituted into artifact.urlTemplate's {os_version}). Optional; defaults to the single supported bookworm line. Each entry is a bare VERSION_ID token.

artifact.description: Default/template artifact for the sysext .raw — how it is fetched and integrity-checked. The URL is templated by OS version (and may carry a '{board}' placeholder) so one descriptor can serve multiple os-release lines and boards; the payload is verified by sha256 AND a detached GPG signature reference before install. Per-board overrides live in boardVariants[] when boards ship genuinely different .raw artifacts.

boardVariants.description: Per-board artifact overrides. Optional. Keys are board_id values (the manifests/boards/<id>.yaml stem). Each value is a full artifact object that REPLACES the top-level artifact when installing on that board — used when boards ship genuinely different .raw payloads (distinct urlTemplate, sha256, sizes). The top-level artifact stays the default/template for any board without an explicit variant. A board named in conditions.boardAllowlist SHOULD have a matching variant here so the manager fetches the right per-board .raw.

conditions.description: Optional capability gate the CeraUI add-on manager evaluates BEFORE any download/merge. Conditions are AND-combined; an absent block means the add-on is always offerable. When ANY condition is unmet the manager MUST NOT crash and MUST NOT install/enable: it leaves the add-on inactive, sets the runtime phase to 'unsupported' (capability can never appear on this hardware/board) or 'pending' (capability may appear later, e.g. an OS upgrade), and records a machine-readable lastError. Canonical lastError reasons: requires_display unmet -> 'addon_requires_display'; requires_gpu_userspace unmet -> 'addon_requires_gpu_userspace'; running board not in boardAllowlist -> 'addon_board_not_allowed'; host VERSION_ID < min_os_version -> 'addon_os_too_old'. These reasons are device-local runtime state (CeraUI AddonStateSchema), not baked into the image.

requires_display.description: When true the add-on only activates on boards with an HDMI/display output (a usable DRM/framebuffer). Boards without display output -> phase 'unsupported', lastError 'addon_requires_display'.

requires_gpu_userspace.description: When true the add-on needs the Mali GPU userspace (libmali, Platform-layer) for EGL/GBM rendering. Boards/images lacking it -> phase 'unsupported', lastError 'addon_requires_gpu_userspace'.

min_os_version.description: Minimum host OS VERSION_ID required (numeric VERSION_ID compare). A host below this -> phase 'pending', lastError 'addon_os_too_old' (an OS upgrade can later satisfy it). Bare VERSION_ID token (e.g. '12' for bookworm).

boardAllowlist.description: Explicit list of board_id values this add-on may activate on. When present the running board MUST be a member; any other board -> phase 'unsupported', lastError 'addon_board_not_allowed'. Board ids are lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens (manifests/boards/<id>.yaml stem).

provides.description: E6/G2 — absolute filesystem paths this sysext contributes. Two axes: (1) collision detection — no two installable add-ons may claim the same path unless they mutually declare each other in conflicts[] (validate-manifests.py E6 check); (2) boundary enforcement — every path MUST live under /usr or /opt, because a systemd-sysext overlays ONLY those subtrees (G2). Paths under /etc or /var are silently lost at merge time and are rejected here.

deps.description: Add-on ids this add-on requires to be installed first. Optional. Resolved by the CeraUI add-on manager before enable.

conflicts.description: Add-on ids that are mutually exclusive with this one. Optional. A pair that mutually lists each other here may legally claim the same provides[] path (they can never be merged together) — that is the provides/conflicts model E6 keys on.

units.description: systemd units the add-on touches at enable/disable time. All three lists are optional. Units are masked-inert by default in the image; the add-on manager unmasks, enables, then starts in that order.

configSchemaRef.description: Optional reference (relative path or URL) to a JSON Schema describing user-supplied configuration for this add-on. When present, CeraUI renders a config form and validates input against it.

validate.description: Optional post-install health probe. The add-on manager runs cmd (argv-only) after merge; a non-zero exit or timeout overrun marks the add-on unhealthy. Add-on health NEVER gates the OTA healthcheck/rollback.

cmd.description: Absolute command (with args) to run as the health probe.

timeout.description: Probe timeout in seconds. Positive integer; defaults to a manager-side value when omitted.

artifact.description: A fetchable, integrity-checked sysext .raw: an HTTPS URL templated by OS version, plus the sha256 + detached GPG signature reference verified before install, and the download/installed sizes used for the space pre-check. Shared by the top-level artifact (default/template) and each boardVariants[] entry (per-board override).

urlTemplate.description: HTTPS URL template for the .raw, parameterised by OS version. MUST contain the literal '{os_version}' placeholder (expanded from compatibleOsVersions at fetch time); may also carry a '{board}' placeholder for the default/template artifact.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the resolved .raw artifact (64 hex chars). Verified post-download before install.

gpgSigRef.description: Reference (URL or relative path) to the detached GPG signature of the artifact, verified against the image-baked CeraLive signing key. Non-empty.

sizeDownload.description: Compressed download size of the .raw in bytes (drives the CeraUI progress/size estimate). Positive integer.

sizeInstalled.description: Installed (merged) size in bytes the extension occupies once active. Positive integer; used for the size/space pre-check.
